data.title: Costco Fee Hike Highlights Membership Strength And Earnings Power Potential
data.description:
• Costco Wholesale (NasdaqGS:COST) has lifted its premium club membership fee, its first increase in seven years.
  • Management reports that renewal rates remain over 92% following the fee change.
  • The move highlights Costco's ability to adjust membership pricing while keeping member engagement high.

Costco runs a global warehouse club model where membership fees are a core profit driver, often more important than product margins. Against a backdrop of rising operating costs and consumers looking for value, the company is leaning on its membership base as a key earnings engine. For you, this fee change with stable renewals highlights how central memberships are to the Costco story.

With renewal rates staying above 92% after the premium fee move, Costco is signaling confidence in its membership value proposition. Investors may watch how membership revenue trends from here and whether management uses future fee adjustments as a recurring tool for earnings at NasdaqGS:COST.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Costco Wholesale by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Costco Wholesale.

2 things going right for Costco Wholesale that this headline doesn't cover.

The premium membership fee increase, with renewal rates still above 92%, underlines how central Costco’s membership economics are to the business model. Membership fees helped deliver US$5.3b in revenue in fiscal 2025 and are described as contributing significantly to income, so a US$10 annual uplift on the premium tier can be meaningful when spread across a large and growing base. With net sales for the first 31 weeks of fiscal 2026 at US$173.26b, up from US$158.87b, Costco is pairing higher fee revenue with ongoing top line growth rather than relying on one lever alone.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Costco Wholesale Narrative

  • The fee increase supports the existing narrative that a sticky membership model and expanding warehouse footprint can keep revenue and earnings growing, even as Costco invests in new perks such as expanded travel deals and lower fuel prices.
  • The slight softening in U.S. and Canada renewal rates mentioned in recent commentary could challenge assumptions that membership trends will always hold near current levels, particularly if future fee hikes arrive more frequently.
  • The potential use of more regular membership fee adjustments as an earnings driver is not fully reflected in the narrative’s focus on warehouse expansion, gas station hours and e commerce growth, so investors may want to factor that in when thinking about Costco’s longer term story.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Costco Wholesale to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ A proposed class action lawsuit related to automatic membership renewal notifications introduces legal and reputational risk at the same time Costco is leaning more on membership economics.
  • ⚠️ Higher wages, supply chain costs and tariff uncertainty could pressure margins, which might limit how much of the additional membership revenue ultimately reaches the bottom line.
  • 🎁 High renewal rates above 92% and US$5.3b in membership fee revenue in 2025 point to a loyal customer base that supports Costco’s wide moat against peers like Walmart’s Sam’s Club and BJ’s Wholesale Club.
  • 🎁 Strong March net sales growth of 11.3% and more than 20% growth in digitally enabled sales suggest Costco is still attracting traffic both in warehouses and online, which can help support the membership value proposition after a fee increase.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it makes sense to watch whether renewal rates stay near current levels over several quarters, not just immediately after the fee change, and how membership revenue tracks relative to overall sales growth. Keep an eye on any updates around the class action over renewal practices, as well as commentary on future fee timing and the role of new perks such as travel deals and low cost fuel in keeping members engaged versus rivals. The balance between rising operating costs and membership driven income will be important for assessing Costco’s execution against other big box and warehouse clubs.

data.title: A Look At Encore Capital Group (ECPG) Valuation As Earnings Growth And Forecast Upgrades Spark Renewed Interest
data.description:
Encore Capital Group (ECPG) has drawn fresh attention after reporting year-over-year earnings per share growth of 117% along with strong sales, regularly coming in ahead of analyst expectations and prompting upward revisions to future earnings forecasts.

See our latest analysis for Encore Capital Group.

Despite a 1 day share price decline of 1.22%, Encore Capital Group’s recent 30 day share price return of 13.07% and 90 day gain of 34.60% sit alongside a very large 1 year total shareholder return of 154.66%. This combination suggests momentum has been building around its earnings story.

Most Popular Narrative: 37.3% Undervalued

Encore Capital Group last closed at $75.43, while the most followed narrative pegs fair value at $120.38, implying a sizeable gap between price and narrative value that puts the focus squarely on what is driving that estimate.

The company's ERC Estimated remaining collections exceeds $5B. Its market capitalization is less than $1b, making its liquidation value more than its trading value. ECPG has stumbled. In its last earnings call, ECPG took a substantial write down, mostly of goodwill related to its Cabot business. It was a management mistake to take write downs multiple times instead of doing it once and moving on. The failure to write down once and be done creates uncertainty and loss of confidence. Management also repeats its well worn slogans about its business. This too is a mistake as it makes investors yawn. The company needs to at least discuss any new initiatives in analytics and AI. At a minimum, it should emphasize whatever it spends on R&D so that the market can gain confidence that ECPG is building a fence around its business and is more than a tired and old debt collection company. The company’s lack of urgency makes it an attractive takeover target. Its business is sound, but its management can be improved.

data.title: Is Rayonier (RYN) Being Priced Fairly After Recent Timber REIT Sentiment Shifts
data.description:
• Wondering whether Rayonier's current share price reflects its true value, or if the market is mispricing this timber and land-focused REIT? This article sets out the key facts so you can judge for yourself.
  • Rayonier's shares last closed at US$21.20, with a 2.0% return over the past week and 5.1% over the past month, while year to date the stock shows a 1.9% decline and a 5.6% decline over the past year.
  • Recent news coverage has mainly centered on Rayonier's positioning within the Specialized REITs space and how timber and land assets fit into diversified income portfolios. Commentary has also highlighted how sentiment toward interest rate expectations and real assets has fed into recent price moves.
  • On Simply Wall St's valuation checks, Rayonier scores 3 out of 6. This raises the question of what different valuation approaches are signaling today, and whether there is an even richer way to think about value that will be covered at the end of this article.

Find out why Rayonier's -5.6% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Rayonier Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth by projecting future funds from operations and then discounting those cash flows back to today’s value.

For Rayonier, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on funds from operations. The latest twelve months free cash flow is given as $652 million. Looking ahead, analysts provide explicit forecasts up to 2028, with free cash flow for that year projected at $497.8 million. Beyond that, Simply Wall St extrapolates further, with ten year projections that rise from $370.1 million in 2026 to $786.8 million in 2035, with each year discounted back to a present value.

Pulling all of these discounted cash flows together, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $44.47 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $21.20, this implies Rayonier is trading at a 52.3% discount to the DCF estimate, which suggests the shares may be materially undervalued on this model alone.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Rayonier is undervalued by 52.3%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 57 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Rayonier.

Approach 2: Rayonier Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about value because it links what you pay per share to the earnings that support that share price. Higher growth expectations and lower perceived risk usually justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually point to a lower, more conservative P/E being seen as fair.

Rayonier currently trades on a P/E of 94.12x. That compares with an average P/E of 16.22x for the Specialized REITs industry and a peer average of 44.46x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” for Rayonier of 54.05x, which is the P/E level suggested after factoring in elements such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and specific risks.

This Fair Ratio is more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or the broad industry because it adjusts for Rayonier’s own characteristics rather than assuming all REITs or all peers deserve similar multiples. Against this yardstick, Rayonier’s actual P/E of 94.12x sits well above the Fair Ratio of 54.05x. On this measure, it reflects a relatively rich earnings multiple.

Result: OVERVALUED

This is where Narratives come in as your way to give a clear story behind the numbers for Rayonier, tying your view on its future revenue, earnings and margins to a Fair Value that can be compared with the current share price.

On Simply Wall St’s Community page, Narratives are an accessible tool used by millions of investors, helping you link a company’s story to a financial forecast and then to a Fair Value that updates automatically when fresh information such as news or earnings is added.

With Rayonier, for example, one Narrative might lean toward the higher Fair Value of about US$32.99, based on expectations for stronger earnings and a future P/E of roughly 27x. Another might sit closer to the lower Fair Value of about US$24 to US$27, assuming more modest revenues and an earnings profile that justifies a different P/E. Comparing any of these Fair Values to the current price can help you decide whether the stock looks closer to fully priced or on sale according to your own story.
